title: Piquant Turkish Steak Tagine
description: A grassy and silky one-pot Turkish dish featuring skirt steak, cooked to perfection in a tandoor clay pot, with a blend of aromatic spices and herbs, perfect for a low-carb friendly dinner option.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the tandoor clay pot over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the skirt steak pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the ground cumin, smoked paprika, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1.5 hours, or until the steak is tender.
Serve the steak and sauce hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
Let the dish rest for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve with a side of roasted vegetables or a salad.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
